frontendStatus: Production frontend built entirely from readable TypeScript source; no recovered application bundle is loaded
architecture: Shared readable TypeScript/NiiVue scientific frontend with browser and Electron desktop targets. Electron owns the private local application server and can create an independent tokenized remote SFTP endpoint on demand. Local loading remains the default; saved non-secret JSON profiles, system SSH authentication in Terminal, and structured SFTP browsing are exposed only when the user chooses a remote source. The profile directory is an initial browsing location rather than a containment boundary; the SSH account and remote operating system enforce access permissions. Remote import and export both use Finder-style SFTP browsers with history, breadcrumbs, direct paths, search, sortable metadata, hidden-file control, and remembered locations. An active Electron remote endpoint is shared reactively with export routing so registration artifacts can be saved either locally or to an SSH-permitted workstation directory using atomic SFTP replacement.
